title: Systems and methods associated with collective contact information
abstract: Methods, systems, and apparatus, including computer programs encoded on computer storage media, for a collective address book system. One of the methods includes: receiving, at the system, a request from a user regarding a topic; selecting automatically, at the system, a potential referrer, the potential referrer selection being based on the likelihood that the potential referrer will respond to the user's request; and communicating automatically with the potential referrer regarding the user's request without the system indicating to the user that the potential referrer has been contacted.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A sy stem compri sing : one or more computers and one or more storage devices on which are stored instructions that are operable, when executed by the one or more computers, to cause the one or more computers to perform operations comprising: (a) receiving, at the system, a request from a user regarding a topic; (b) selecting automatically, at the system, a potential referrer, the potential referrer selection being based on the likelihood that the potential referrer will respond to the user's request; and (c) communicating automatically with the potential referrer regarding the user's request without the system indicating to the user that the potential referrer has been contacted. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the topic is a person of interest. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the selection of the potential referrer is based on the potential referrer 's relationship to the user and the person of interest. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the selection of the potential referrer is based on the potential referrer' s prior response rate to referral requests. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the selection of the potential referrer is based on the potential referrer' s availability at the time of selection. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein selecting a potential referrer comprises selecting substantially concurrently a plurality of potential referrers. 7. The system of claim 6, wherein communicating with the potential referrer comprises communicating substantially concurrently with the plurality of potential referrers. 8. The system of claim 6, wherein communicating with the potential referrer comprises communicating with the plurality of potential referrers according to an order. 9. The system of claim 1, the operations further comprising (a) receiving, at the system, a response from the referrer; and 

 (b) informing the user of the identity of the referrer. 10. A computer-implemented method in a collective address system, the method comprising: (a) receiving, at the system, a request from a user regarding a topic; (b) selecting automatically, at the system, a potential referrer, the potential referrer selection being based on the likelihood that the potential referrer will respond to the user's request; and (c) communicating automatically with the potential referrer regarding the user's request without the system indicating to the user that the potential referrer has been contacted. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein the topic is a person of interest. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, wherein the selection of the potential referrer is based on the potential referrer's relationship to the user and the person of interest. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein the selection of the potential referrer is based on the potential referrer's prior response rate to referral requests. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein the selection of the potential referrer is based on the potential referrer's availability at the time of selection. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein selecting a potential referrer comprises selecting substantially concurrently a plurality of potential referrers. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 15, wherein communicating with the potential referrer comprises communicating substantially concurrently with the plurality of potential referrers. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 15, wherein communicating with the potential referrer comprises communicating with the plurality of potential referrers according to an order. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, the method further comprising (a) receiving, at the system, a response from the referrer; and (b) informing the user of the identity of the referrer. 19. The computer-implemented method of claim 11 , wherein the person of interest can be someone other than a user of the method. 20. One or more computer-readable non-transitory storage media encoded with instructions that, when executed by one or more computers, cause the one or more computers to perform operations comprising: (a) receiving, at the system, a request from a first user regarding a second user of the system; (b) selecting automatically, at the system, a referrer, the referrer selection being based on the likelihood that the referrer will respond to the first user's request; and (c) communicating automatically with the referrer regarding the first user's request without the system indicating to the first user that the referrer has been contacted.
